req.title: Open checking account (boundary variant)
req.statement: A verified customer shall be able to open a checking account with an initial deposit between $25 and $50,000; deposits outside this range shall be rejected. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-026 defines a deposit range [25, 50000] with explicit boundary behaviour and two error pathways (422 for out-of-range deposit, 403 for unverified customer). I apply: EP to cover valid mid-range deposit and below-min deposit; BVA to probe exact min (25) and exact max (50000) thresholds; NEG for the unverified-customer pathway; DT to combine verified=false with an otherwise-valid deposit, confirming the 403 guard fires before range validation. Each test uses a unique idempotent customer ID generated via uuid4 and cleans up via a delete fixture to stay deterministic.

tests.name: test_open_checking_valid_mid_range_deposit
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer token and deposit 5000
trace: R-FIN-026: valid deposit in [25, 50000] must create account
R-FIN-026: welcome-notification must be dispatched on success
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_valid_mid_range_deposit(verified_customer_token, api_client, cleanup_account):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 5000}
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/accounts/checking",
        json=payload,
        headers=<auth_header>(verified_customer_token),
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body, "account_id missing from response"
    assert body.get("status") == "active"
    assert body.get("notification_sent") is True
    cleanup_account(body["account_id"])

tests.name: test_open_checking_deposit_below_minimum_rejected
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer token and deposit 10
expected: Response body contains error code DEPOSIT_OUT_OF_RANGE or equivalent
trace: R-FIN-026: deposit < 25 must be rejected with 422

tests.name: test_open_checking_deposit_above_maximum_rejected
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer token and deposit 60000
expected: Response body contains error code DEPOSIT_OUT_OF_RANGE or equivalent
trace: R-FIN-026: deposit > 50000 must be rejected with 422

tests.name: test_open_checking_exact_minimum_deposit_accepted
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer token and deposit 25
trace: R-FIN-026: boundary behaviour at exact min threshold 25 must succeed
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_exact_minimum_deposit_accepted(verified_customer_token, api_client, cleanup_account):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 25}
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/accounts/checking",
        json=payload,
        headers=<auth_header>(verified_customer_token),
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 at min boundary, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert body.get("status") == "active"
    assert body.get("notification_sent") is True
    cleanup_account(body["account_id"])

actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer token and deposit 24
trace: R-FIN-026: deposit one unit below min boundary (24) must be rejected with 422

tests.name: test_open_checking_exact_maximum_deposit_accepted
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer token and deposit 50000
trace: R-FIN-026: boundary behaviour at exact max threshold 50000 must succeed
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_exact_maximum_deposit_accepted(verified_customer_token, api_client, cleanup_account):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 50000}
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/accounts/checking",
        json=payload,
        headers=<auth_header>(verified_customer_token),
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 at max boundary, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert body.get("status") == "active"
    assert body.get("notification_sent") is True
    cleanup_account(body["account_id"])

tests.name: test_open_checking_one_above_maximum_rejected
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer token and deposit 50001
trace: R-FIN-026: deposit one unit above max boundary (50001) must be rejected with 422

tests.name: test_open_checking_unverified_customer_forbidden
actions: POST /accounts/checking with unverified customer token and deposit 5000
trace: R-FIN-026: customer.verified=false must yield 403 regardless of deposit amount

tests.name: test_open_checking_unverified_customer_deposit_below_min_returns_403_not_422
preconditions: initial_deposit = 10 (both auth guard and range guard triggered)
actions: POST /accounts/checking with unverified customer token and deposit 10
expected: HTTP 403 Forbidden (auth guard takes precedence over range validation)
trace: R-FIN-026: when both verified=false AND deposit<25, auth check (403) must take precedence over range check (422)

tests.name: test_open_checking_state_transition_pending_to_active
preconditions: Account does not yet exist for this customer reference
actions: POST /accounts/checking to create account (expect transition: none -> pending -> active)
GET /accounts/{account_id} to confirm final state is active
expected: POST returns HTTP 201 with status=active or status=pending
trace: R-FIN-026: successful account opening must drive account to active state
R-FIN-026: welcome-notification dispatched implies active state
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_state_transition_pending_to_active(verified_customer_token, api_client, cleanup_account):
    create_resp = api_client.post(
        "/accounts/checking",
        json={"initial_deposit": 1000},
        headers=<auth_header>(verified_customer_token),
    )
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    account_id = create_resp.json()["account_id"]
    get_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}",
        headers=<auth_header>(verified_customer_token),
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json().get("status") == "active", "Account must reach active state"
    cleanup_account(account_id)
